events.text: Patient is a 67-year-old female of White/Caucasian race, Hispanic ethnicity, with a BMI of 30.2 and a past medical history of type 2 diabetes, obesity, and a family history of breast cancer in her sister (diagnosed at age 52).

At age 67, patient presented with progressive epigastric and left upper quadrant pain, nausea, and 10-pound weight loss; CT revealed a 5.1 cm mass in the body and tail of the pancreas (C25.1) with encasement of the celiac artery and splenic artery, abutment of the SMA, and suspicious peripancreatic lymphadenopathy; classified as locally advanced unresectable.

At age 67, CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreas protocol showed a 5.1 cm heterogeneous mass spanning the pancreatic body and tail with celiac artery and splenic artery encasement and abutment of the SMA greater than 180 degrees; peripancreatic lymph nodes measuring up to 1.8 cm; no definite hepatic metastases; no ascites; mild splenomegaly likely secondary to splenic vein compression.

At age 67, EUS with FNA performed; mass measured 5.3 x 4.1 cm involving pancreatic body and tail; celiac trunk encasement confirmed; three passes obtained with 22-gauge needle with rapid on-site evaluation confirming malignant cells.

At age 67, EUS-guided FNA of pancreatic body/tail mass: poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology code 8140/3) with high nuclear grade, frequent mitotic figures, and areas of necrosis; immunohistochemistry positive for CK7, negative for CK20; SMAD4 lost by immunohistochemistry; final diagnosis: poorly differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

At age 67, next-generation sequencing on FNA specimen: KRAS G12V mutation (variant allele frequency 52%), TP53 R248W mutation, SMAD4 deletion, CDKN2A p16 loss, BRCA1 wild-type, BRCA2 wild-type, PALB2 wild-type, MSI-stable (MSS), tumor mutational burden 5 mutations/Mb, HER2/ERBB2 not amplified.

At age 67, CA 19-9 at diagnosis was 2,340 U/mL; CEA 14.2 ng/mL; clinical staging cT4N1M0, Stage III; ECOG performance status 1; multidisciplinary tumor board recommended systemic chemotherapy with FOLFIRINOX given adequate performance status; palliative intent discussed with patient and family.

At age 67, pain management plan initiated with long-acting oxycodone and gabapentin for neuropathic component of pain; referral to palliative care team for symptom management; nutritional counseling and pancreatic enzyme supplementation started.

At age 67, initiated FOLFIRINOX (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 12 cycles; port-a-cath placed for venous access.

At age 67, after cycle 1 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 3 nausea and grade 2 vomiting despite three-drug antiemetic prophylaxis; required 48-hour hospital admission for IV hydration and antiemetic management.

At age 67, after cycle 4 of FOLFIRINOX, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia with ANC 0.6 x 10^9/L and grade 2 diarrhea; subsequent cycles dose-reduced with irinotecan decreased by 25% and pegfilgrastim added.
At age 67, CA 19-9 after 4 cycles of FOLFIRINOX decreased to 987 U/mL from 2,340 U/mL; partial biochemical response.

At age 67, restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFIRINOX showed decrease in pancreatic body/tail mass from 5.1 cm to 3.8 cm; persistent celiac artery encasement; SMA abutment stable; no new distant metastases; peripancreatic lymph nodes decreased in size.
At age 67, continued modified FOLFIRINOX through cycle 10 with ongoing dose reductions; cumulative grade 2 peripheral neuropathy developed; oxaliplatin discontinued after cycle 8.
At age 68, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with significant numbness in feet affecting balance and daily activities; oxaliplatin permanently discontinued.

At age 68, following completion of 10 cycles of chemotherapy, patient received SBRT (stereotactic body radiation therapy) to the pancreatic body/tail mass, 33 Gy in 5 fractions over 10 days with fiducial marker-guided treatment; concurrent low-dose capecitabine as radiosensitizer.

At age 68, restaging CT 8 weeks post-SBRT showed further decrease in pancreatic mass to 2.9 cm with increased central necrosis; CA 19-9 further decreased to 312 U/mL; stable celiac encasement; no distant disease.

At age 68, surveillance CT at 4 months post-SBRT demonstrated three new hypodense hepatic lesions in the right lobe measuring 1.2 cm, 0.8 cm, and 0.6 cm, suspicious for metastatic disease; CA 19-9 rose to 1,890 U/mL.

At age 68, confirmed disease progression with new hepatic metastases; restaged as cT4N1M1, Stage IV; discussed prognosis and goals of care with patient; decision to proceed with second-line chemotherapy.
At age 68, initiated second-line gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 with nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles; palliative intent.
At age 68, after cycle 2 of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, patient developed grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 alopecia, and worsening grade 2 neuropathy; nab-paclitaxel dose reduced to 100 mg/m2.

At age 68, palliative care team managing increasing pain with rotation to hydromorphone and fentanyl patch; appetite stimulant (megestrol acetate) added; patient maintaining ECOG performance status 2; weight 158 lbs, down from 172 lbs at diagnosis.

scenario_blurb: Locally advanced or borderline resectable pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Treated with neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX, some achieving resectability and undergoing Whipple procedure, some with disease progression. Include CA 19-9 monitoring, CT imaging response assessment.
